commit.message:
feat: intelligent reminder lead time based on task type

- Meeting/appointment: remind 30 min before (not at start time)
- Payment/bills: remind 3 days before
- Deadline/submission: remind 1 day before
- Travel/flight: remind 2 hours before
- Medical appointment: remind 1 hour before
- Other timed tasks: remind 15 min before
- No time specified: remind at 9am on due date

Also: reminder confirmation always shows actual remind time vs due time

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Dockerfile.prod for Render deployment

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add FastVLM-0.5B and FastVLM-7B; fix FastVLM inference implementation

All three FastVLM variants now share a _load_fastvlm() helper using the
correct inference pattern from the model cards:
- AutoTokenizer (not AutoProcessor)
- Manual IMAGE_TOKEN_INDEX (-200) insertion per frame
- model.get_vision_tower().image_processor() for pixel values
- images=px passed directly to model.generate()

The previous load_fastvlm (1.5B) used AutoProcessor which was incorrect.

New keys: fastvlm_0b5 (~1 GB), fastvlm_7b (~16 GB).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: make Meta OAuth resilient for testers without IG Business pages

fetchInstagramAccount was fatal — if a tester had no Facebook page with
an Instagram Business account linked, the entire OAuth callback returned
502 and the token was never saved. Now it's non-fatal (like fetchAdAccount),
the token is always persisted, and the user can fill in Account ID manually.

Also adds missing OAuth scopes (instagram_content_publish,
instagram_manage_comments, pages_read_engagement, pages_manage_posts)
and exposes has_token/needs_manual_config in the API response.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Improve 40+ connectors: feature expansions, formatting, and test coverage

Major feature expansions:
- DocuSign: enriched envelope, recipient, signing, and tracking modules
  with expanded field handling and validation (~543 lines across 4 modules)
- Kubernetes: significantly expanded connector, events, zone management,
  and client with improved resource handling; integration tests expanded
  from ~300 to ~700+ lines
- Zendesk: major SLA module expansion (~527 lines), enhanced analytics
  and categorization with additional metric types and classification logic
- Sentry: expanded alert rules and triage logic with richer analysis
- Reddit: improved client, messaging, moderation, posting, research,
  saved, and content modules with better API coverage
- Terraform: enhanced plan, drift detection, apply, import, modules,
  safety checks, and state management across all 8 submodules
- Spotify: refined playback command validation formatting, history
  tracking, search, and client improvements
- Twilio: expanded connector and client with additional messaging/voice
  capabilities and test coverage
- Home Assistant: enriched integration module
- Salesforce: expanded connector operations
- Figma: enhanced connector with additional design operations
- Arxiv: improved CASS integration module
- Airtable: expanded attachments, records, and webhook handling
- HubSpot: enriched connector and client
- Jira: expanded client API methods

Formatting and minor improvements (rustfmt compliance):
- 1Password, Algolia, Amplitude, Asana, BigQuery, Bitbucket, Box,
  ClickUp, Dropbox, DuckDB, Elasticsearch, Evernote, GitLab, Grafana,
  Intercom, LinkedIn, Logseq, Mailchimp, Make, MCP-Bridge, Metabase,
  Mixpanel, Monday, MongoDB, N8N, PandaDoc, PostHog, Pulumi, Retool,
  Roam, Salesforce, Segment, SendGrid, Snowflake, Todoist, Trello,
  Twitter streaming, Zapier — formatting corrections, method chain
  compaction, multi-line assertion restructuring

fcp-store: object store and error handling refinements.

commit.message:
Add OneNote and Word document support to Microsoft 365 connector

New OneNote integration (connectors/microsoft365/src/onenote.rs):
- ListNotebooksInput, ListSectionsInput, ListPagesInput for notebook
  hierarchy traversal
- GetPageInput, GetPageContentInput for retrieving page content
- CreatePageInput, UpdatePageInput for creating and modifying pages
- OneNotePageContent structured response type
- Full CRUD operations wired through the connector dispatch

Word document support (connector.rs, client.rs):
- ListWordDocumentsInput: enumerate .docx files in user drive paths
- GetWordDocumentInput: retrieve document metadata (WordDocumentMetadata)
- ExtractWordTextInput: extract plain text from .docx via Open XML
  parsing (quick_xml + zip crates), with configurable max_chars
  (default 20k, limit 100k) and 8MB download guard
- CreateWordDocumentInput: create new .docx with content, using simple
  upload for <4MB or chunked upload for larger files
- UpdateWordDocumentInput: replace document content
- ExportWordDocumentInput: export to PDF/HTML via Graph API content
  endpoint, with 25MB export guard
- WORD_EXTRACT_* and WORD_EXPORT_* safety constants

Infrastructure:
- Cargo.toml: added quick-xml and zip dependencies

commit.message:
feat: add Vercel Speed Insights

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Expand FCP Specification V3 and update README with current architecture topology

FCP_Specification_V3.md: major expansion of the audit chain specification
(section 6.3) — AuditEvent now includes correlation_id, actor principal,
event classification (AuditEventClass enum with Decision, Execution,
SecretUse, Approval, ZoneTransfer, Lease, Checkpoint, Revocation,
SupplyChain, Repair, Incident), disposition tracking (AuditDisposition
enum), subject references, decision/operation receipt linkage, checkpoint
and lease IDs, revocation head linkage, structured summary with
reason_code/coverage_bps/quorum_met/external_target, and degraded_mode
flag. Establishes audit as the normative tamper-evidence system rather
than optional observability, with exactly one accepted audit lineage
per zone at any head_seq.

README.md: updated crate directory tree to reflect current workspace
topology including fcp-cbor, fcp-crypto, fcp-store, fcp-streaming,
fcp-oauth, fcp-graphql, fcp-google-discovery, fcp-telemetry, fcp-testkit,
fcp-e2e, fcp-host, fcp-bootstrap, fcp-ratelimit, and fwc. Added
"Current Implementation Topology" table mapping crates into responsibility
bands (semantic core, durable mesh/data plane, host/operator surfaces,
connector authoring/runtime helpers, evidence/test harnesses). Added
"FCP3 Ownership Direction" section documenting the convergence toward
strict one-concept-one-home boundaries for kernel, mesh, host, SDK,
and tooling layers.

commit.message:
Fix MapEditor stale DB overlay, duplicate move handles, and reduce shape rendering duplication

- Fix stale DB overlay: rebuildDbChangesOverlay wasn't called when changes
  were seeded into or removed from local pendingChanges, causing duplicate
  rendering and visual disappearance bugs. Added explicit rebuildDbOverlay()
  calls at all seed/un-seed points.
- Fix incomplete skip logic: Update-type seeded changes (keyed by entity Id)
  were never filtered from the DB overlay, only Create-type (keyed by -change.id).
- Fix Rectangle move handle duplication: editing.enable() after drag recreated
  Leaflet-Draw's built-in _moveMarker; now re-removed after every drag.
- Extract createLeafletShape() utility to consolidate duplicated Circle/Rectangle/
  Polygon creation across createShapeLayer, createPendingAddLayer, and
  rebuildDbChangesOverlay (~55 lines removed).

commit.message:
Enable Codex auto-resume by default — attach to active threads

The codex app-server subprocess only forwards approval requests for
threads it has attached to via thread/resume.  Auto-resume was gated
behind DECKY_CODEX_AUTO_RESUME=1, so the subprocess sat idle and never
received approval events.  Now auto-resume defaults to on, using
process.cwd() for thread selection.  Set DECKY_CODEX_AUTO_RESUME=0
to disable.

commit.message:
fix: move _orgs block to end of registry in seed script

The _orgs block was being added incrementally inside the loop, causing
subsequent org sections to appear after it. Since restore/sync scripts
break at _orgs:, this meant non-first orgs were never processed.
Now strips any existing _orgs block before seeding and appends it
once at the very end with all orgs deduplicated.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
